back_translation: And it happened at the return of the year, at the time of the going out of kings, that David sent Joab and his servants with him and all Israel, and they destroyed the sons of Ammon and besieged Rabbah; but David remained in Jerusalem.

And it happened at the time of evening, that David arose from his bed and walked on the roof of the king's house; and he saw from the roof a woman bathing; and the woman was very beautiful of appearance.
And David sent and inquired about the woman, and he said, 'Is this not Bathsheba daughter of Eliam, the wife of Uriah the Hittite?'
And David sent messengers and took her, and she came to him, and he lay with her — and she had been purifying herself from her uncleanness — and she returned to her house.
And the woman conceived, and sent and told David, and said, 'I am pregnant.'
And David sent to Joab, 'Send to me Uriah the Hittite.' And Joab sent Uriah to David.
And Uriah came to him; and David asked about the welfare of Joab, and the welfare of the people, and the welfare of the war.
And David said to Uriah, 'Go down to your house and wash your feet.' And Uriah went out from the king's house, and a gift from the king followed after him.
And Uriah slept at the entrance of the king's house with all the servants of his lord, and did not go down to his house.
And they told David, saying, 'Uriah did not go down to his house.' And David said to Uriah, 'Have you not come from a journey? Why have you not gone down to your house?'

And Uriah said to David, 'The ark and Israel and Judah are dwelling in tents, and my lord Joab and the servants of my lord are camped on the face of the field — and shall I come to my house to eat and to drink and to lie with my wife? As you live, and as your soul lives, I will not do this thing.'
And David said to Uriah, 'Stay here also today, and tomorrow I will send you.' And Uriah stayed in Jerusalem that day and the next.
And David called to him, and he ate before him and drank, and he made him drunk; and he went out in the evening to lie on his bed with the servants of his lord, and to his house he did not go down.
And it happened in the morning, that David wrote a letter to Joab and sent it by the hand of Uriah.
And he wrote in the letter, saying, 'Give Uriah at the front of the faces of the strong battle, and withdraw from after him, that he may be struck and die.'
And it happened, when Joab watched the city, that he gave Uriah to the place where he knew that mighty men were.
And the men of the city went out and fought with Joab, and some of the people fell, of the servants of David; and Uriah the Hittite also died.
And Joab sent and told David all the words of the battle.
And he commanded the messenger, saying, 'When you have finished telling the king all the words of the battle,
and it shall be, if the king's anger rises and he says to you, Why did you draw near to the city to fight? Did you not know that they would shoot from above the wall?

Who struck Abimelech son of Jerubbesheth? Did not a woman throw on him a piece of millstone from above the wall, and he died at Thebez? Why did you draw near to the wall? — then you shall say, Also your servant Uriah the Hittite is dead.'
And the messenger went, and came and told David all that Joab had sent him.
And the messenger said to David, 'Because the men prevailed over us, and came out to us in the field; and we were upon them as far as the entrance of the gate.
And the archers shot at your servants from above the wall, and some of the servants of the king died; and also your servant Uriah the Hittite is dead.'

And David said to the messenger, 'Thus you shall say to Joab: Do not let this matter be evil in your eyes, for thus and thus the sword devours; strengthen your battle against the city and overthrow it. And strengthen him.'
And the wife of Uriah heard that Uriah her husband was dead, and she lamented over her husband.
And the mourning passed, and David sent and received her to his house, and she became to him a wife and bore to him a son. But the thing that David did was evil in the eyes of the LORD.
